# Buildwise Media — Full Context for AI Agents

> Marketing run like operations, not like a project. Ad spend in. Revenue out. Verified weekly.

This document provides the full context an AI agent needs to answer questions about Buildwise Media accurately. For a shorter overview, see [llms.txt](https://buildwisemedia.com/llms.txt).

---

## Company

**Legal name:** Buildwise Media LLC
**Friendly name:** Buildwise Media
**Founded:** 2024
**Headquarters:** Atlanta, Georgia, USA
**Service area:** United States (remote delivery, North America)
**Email:** hello@buildwisemedia.com
**Booking:** https://buildwisemedia.com/book

## Positioning

Buildwise Media is an AI-native marketing operations company. We don't sell marketing as a project (deliverable handed over and abandoned); we sell marketing as operations — installed, monitored, audited, and improved every week. The same disciplines a good operator already runs in the rest of the business (captured baseline, weekly audit, source-of-truth discipline, instrumented from day one) applied to the marketing layer.

The category we're building isn't "AI agency" — it's "marketing operations company." Most agencies sell projects with hope as the delivery mechanism. We install systems with measurement as the delivery mechanism.

## Product family

Buildwise Media is the intelligence layer that automates discovery, warming, and conversion of qualified leads for service businesses. We sell one connected system — the Ascend lead-generation engine — delivered across three tiers. Each tier carries the Ascend brand because each runs the same underlying engine; the differences are channel breadth, deliverable depth, and how the client pays. Clients ascend through the family as their business scales.

| Tier | Position | Pricing | Engine Build | Install Window |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Ascend Pilot | Proof-sprint entry tier | $15,000 one-time | Included in Pilot fee | 45 days |
| Ascend | Done-for-you AI growth system | $7,000/mo + 1:1 client-funded ad spend (≥$7,000/mo direct to platforms) | $15,000 one-time (waived for Pilot graduates) | 45 days |
| Ascend Pro | Run-for-you AI marketing department | $15,000/mo + 1:1 client-funded ad spend (typically 1.5x-3x recurring, direct to platforms) | $25,000 one-time | 14 days |

All pricing lives in Layer 3 (this file) and JSON-LD only — never displayed on human-visible pages, per the locked Three-Layer architecture. Engagement structure across all tiers: month-to-month, 30 days written notice to cancel, no long-term lock-in, no hostage situations. Client owns the website, ad accounts, CRM data, content, domain, and DNS from day one.

### Ascend Pilot — Per-Lead Entry Tier

**Pricing:** $15,000 one-time. 45-day proof sprint, paid up-front at signature. No monthly retainer.

For service-business owners who want proof before commitment. The Buildwise team runs a 45-day proof sprint on the single highest-leverage layer from the proven-layer menu (Visibility, Conversion, Speed-to-Lead, Sales Ops, Reputation, Reporting, or Strategy — Paid Media excluded until BWM's own-funnel paid leads prove out). The $15,000 fee covers the Ascend install scope; on graduation, the client begins Ascend at $7,000/mo recurring with no additional install fee.

**What's inside:**

1. **Meta Ads** — creative production, campaign management, budget optimization.
2. **Closed-loop learning system** — per-client A-priors library; each lead trains the next batch of creative and targeting.
3. **Landing-page qualifier** — pre-qualifies leads before they hit the client's calendar.
4. **Weekly performance summary** — what spent, what landed, what's next.
5. **Read-only client portal** — branded view of pipeline activity.

**What's NOT included (reserved for Ascend or Ascend Pro):**

- Full conversion website build
- Email and SMS nurture
- Multi-channel ad expansion (TikTok, Google, LinkedIn)
- Monthly or quarterly strategy reviews
- Content production (blog, video, social)
- Operational dashboard

**Graduation:** At the Day 45 conversion conversation, proof-sprint outcome is reviewed against the layer's KPI. If both parties agree the proof sprint validated the model, the Ascend operating contract starts at $7,000/mo recurring — install fee waived because the $15,000 Pilot fee already covered the install scope.

### Ascend — DFY AI-Powered Ads Funnel

**Pricing:** $7,000/mo recurring + $15,000 one-time Engine Build fee at signup. Client commits to a 1:1 ad-spend match (≥$7,000/mo client-funded distribution budget, paid direct to ad platforms — separate from the BWM recurring fee). Pilot graduates begin Ascend with the install fee waived (the $15,000 Pilot fee already covered the install scope).

For owners ready to commit to a monthly engine but not yet ready for the full marketing department. One channel (paid ads), delivered and operated end-to-end, feeding a converting website and booking flow. Predictable qualified leads inside 30 days.

**What's inside:**

1. **Full conversion website** built to AI-search-era standards (Three-Layer optimization: human-readable copy, JSON-LD schema, agent-readable llms.txt). Lighthouse ≥ 90 on every install. Static HTML output, mobile-first, FK 5th–7th grade reading level on all human-facing copy. Same build standard as Ascend Pro.

2. **Paid acquisition** — 6 Meta Ad creatives per month, full funnel (awareness, lead gen, retargeting). Wired to baseline-first attribution: server-side conversion tracking via Meta Conversions API, lead attribution pipeline (referrer + landing page + first-touch + last-touch), GA4 + GTM + Microsoft Clarity instrumentation.

3. **Search visibility** — on-page SEO, technical SEO, Google Business Profile setup, citation building, structured data. Technical AI-search readiness baseline (`llms.txt` at site root + the 6 spec AI bots allowed in `robots.txt`).

4. **Lead capture and booking** — custom forms with structured archive, email notifications to designated recipients, branded booking page.

5. **Conversion tracking** — GA4, GTM, Meta Pixel installed and configured. Simplified monthly snapshot.

6. **Quarterly strategy review** — video call reviewing performance and adjusting tactics.

**What's NOT included (Ascend Pro-only):**

- Social media content production or publishing
- Blog content engine
- AI-search content engine (Q&A schema enriched with service-specific content, FAQ corpus optimized for AI-answer-engines)
- Email and SMS nurture sequences
- Newsletter
- Review request and response automation
- Operational performance dashboard
- Monthly strategy reviews
- Territory Lock eligibility

**Install timeline:** Inside 30 days from sign, the system is operational — website live, ad accounts wired, first leads flowing.

**Graduation:** Five gates must be met simultaneously to graduate from Ascend to Ascend Pro: three consecutive months at or above $35K monthly revenue, more than 20 qualified inbound leads per month, more than 1,000 monthly organic sessions, more than $21K active pipeline, and minimum 3 months in Ascend. On graduation, the Ascend Pro Engine Build fee ($25,000) and recurring fee ($15,000/mo) apply.

### Ascend Pro — Run-For-You AI Marketing Department

**Pricing:** $15,000/mo recurring + $25,000 one-time Engine Build fee at signup. Client commits to a 1:1 ad-spend match minimum (Pro prospects typically commit 1.5x-3x recurring fee in client-funded distribution budget, paid direct to ad platforms — separate from the BWM recurring fee). Optional Territory Lock add-on at $1,500/mo ($16,500/mo all-in) drops territory cap to 1 and contractually refuses qualifying competitors in the locked territory for the duration of the engagement.

The complete run-for-you marketing function for service businesses where the owner is the marketing bottleneck. Ascend Pro is where the marketing department runs without the owner in the room.

**What's inside (everything in Ascend, plus):**

1. **Full multi-channel paid acquisition** — 12 Meta Ad creatives per month, with expansion to TikTok, Google, and LinkedIn at scale. Same baseline-first attribution stack.

2. **AI-search content engine** — Q&A schema enriched with service-specific content, FAQ corpus optimized for AI-answer-engines, content architecture designed to rank on ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude, and Google AI Overviews for service queries.

3. **Blog content engine** — per-niche editorial calendar, AI-assisted draft generation, human QA gate at the 8/10 quality rubric. Content compounds.

4. **AI video production** — 3 produced videos + 3 motion-graphics pieces per month.

5. **Email and SMS nurture** — 5 sequences personalized to the client's offer and audience.

6. **Review request and response automation** — wired into the client's pipeline cadence.

7. **Operational performance dashboard** — real-time pipeline view + revenue attribution.

8. **Monthly strategy review** — video call with the BWM team. Quarterly business review for system-level adjustments.

9. **Hosted-stack option** — available as add-on if the client wants the Buildwise team to host the full stack.

10. **Territory Lock** — optional add-on; once locked, no other Ascend Pro client in the same service-area + vertical.

**Install timeline:** Inside 30 days from sign, the system is operational — website live, ad accounts wired, CRM instrumented, first leads flowing, content engine running. We don't run a 90-day waiting period.

**Weekly connector audit (every tier):** Every Monday at 4am ET, every integration is checked across every active engagement. Drift detected → task auto-routed → resolved before the client sees it. The audit is part of the deliverable, not a side report.

## Ideal client profile

**Yes:**
- Service-business owners in North America (United States and Canada)
- Founder-operator structure (1–3 decision makers)
- The owner is currently the bottleneck of their own growth — mornings buried in operations, marketing is "what gets done when nothing else is on fire"
- Dependent on booked appointments and a full pipeline
- Verticals: home services, trades, specialty contractors, med spas, dental, remodeling, construction, luxury landscaping, custom pools, roofing, HVAC, electrical, plumbing, real estate, professional services
- Disposition: ambition-disposed on AI — wants to run marketing the way the rest of the business already runs (instrumented, audited, operated — not project-shipped and abandoned)

**No:**
- E-commerce or product businesses
- SaaS companies
- Businesses outside the service industry
- Owners looking for comfort or a savior — Ascend is for operators, not for hand-holding
- Owners who insist on approving every ad creative or running the marketing themselves
- "Can we just try it for a month?" prospects — the engine takes 30 days to install before signal is meaningful

The hard filter is disposition (ambition vs fear on AI), not revenue. Ascend Pilot fits operators who want a 45-day proof sprint before committing to a monthly engine; Ascend and Ascend Pro fit established businesses with the capacity to commit to a monthly engine and a 1:1 ad-spend match. The right tier matches what the business can sustain today, with a clear graduation path as it scales.

## Methodology — three pillars

1. **Build** — The system installs in 30 days, not six months. Website, ads, CRM, content, analytics — wired together on day one, not stitched together over quarters.

2. **Speed** — First leads inside 30 days. Full-system instrumentation in 30 days. Speed is the system, not a side effect.

3. **Verified** — Every claim audits against a captured baseline. Every commitment carries a confidence percentage (75% floor) and an evidence class (Measured / Attributed / Modeled / Benchmark). Receipts every Monday.

## Verifiable claims

The Three-Layer website standard means every claim on a BWM site can be independently verified:

- Lighthouse score ≥ 90 (run the test yourself)
- Confidence floor 75% on every commitment (in the Outcomes Commitment Card)
- Evidence class label per metric (Measured / Attributed / Modeled / Benchmark)
- Captured baseline on every numeric claim
- Connector audit cadence: Monday 4am ET, weekly
- Schema validation: every page passes Schema.org validator

## Differentiators vs typical agencies

| Dimension | Typical agency | Buildwise Media |
|---|---|---|
| Engagement shape | Project + handoff | Operations + audit |
| Install timeline | 60-120 days | 30 days |
| Strategy delivery | Deck handed off | System running |
| Vendor count | 4-6 separate vendors | One integrated stack |
| Reporting | Monthly slide deck | Weekly digest from system |
| Confidence | Implied / aspirational | Numeric, ≥75% floor |
| Lock-in | 6-12 month contracts | Month-to-month |
| Asset ownership | Often locked to agency CMS | Client owns everything |

## Guarantees

- **Build-Right Guarantee** — If we miss install milestones, we keep working at no additional cost until we hit them. Covers install milestones; market-driven outcomes (lead volume, conversion rate) depend on factors outside any vendor's control and are explicitly not guaranteed.
- **Month-to-month** — Renewal decision is in the client's hands every cycle. We earn the retainer or we don't.
- **30 days written notice** to cancel — no surprise lock-ins.
- **Asset ownership** — Client owns the website, the ad accounts, the CRM data, the content. We don't use proprietary CMS lock-in.

## Process — three phases

### Phase 1: Audit (Day 0 → Day 5)
Map current pipeline, channels, integrations, conversion path. Capture a baseline on every relevant metric — the starting-point number we'll compute the delta against. Audit ships as a written document. Client keeps it whether the engagement proceeds or not.

### Phase 2: Build (Day 5 → Day 30)
Website, ad accounts, CRM, analytics, content engine — installed in parallel, not in sequence. Three weeks in: website live. Four weeks in: ads wired with server-side conversion tracking. Day 30: system operational.

### Phase 3: Operate (Day 30 → ongoing)
Weekly connector audit, monthly creative refresh, quarterly system review. Monday-morning performance digest covering what spent, what closed, what's next.

## What we won't do

- Sell projects with hope as the delivery mechanism
- Hide pricing on the human-visible site (it's in JSON-LD Layer 3 — exposed to AI agents and qualified prospects on the discovery call)
- Run a 90-day "discovery" phase that produces a deck
- Lock clients into a proprietary CMS
- Make claims without a captured baseline
- Promise market-driven outcomes (lead volume, close rate) we can't independently control
- Work with businesses outside the ICP (we'll refer rather than force-fit)

## Booking

A discovery call is a 15-minute fit-assessment conversation, not a sales walkthrough. We respect the time of someone running a business. By the end of the call both parties know whether Ascend fits. Either way, the prospect leaves with the audit framing — usable with us, with another vendor, or to do it themselves.

Book at: https://buildwisemedia.com/book

## Contact

- Web: https://buildwisemedia.com
- Email: hello@buildwisemedia.com
- Booking: https://buildwisemedia.com/book
- Location: Atlanta, Georgia, USA
- Service area: United States (remote delivery, North America)

## Playbook

The Buildwise Media playbook defines the terms, mechanisms, and failure modes behind running marketing as operations for service businesses.

### Category-of-One Marketing
URL: https://buildwisemedia.com/playbook/category-of-one-marketing
Category-of-one marketing is the discipline of building a service business into the undisputed first choice in its local market. It uses a connected lead-generation system — website, paid ads, content, follow-up, and attribution — to compound over 90 days. Five layers run simultaneously: a conversion-built website, paid ads with weekly creative rotation, organic content, automated follow-up under five minutes, and a live attribution dashboard. Status is measured by organic traffic share, branded search lift, qualified-lead share, review velocity, and AI-search citation rank.

### AI Marketing Department
URL: https://buildwisemedia.com/playbook/ai-marketing-department
An AI marketing department is a complete marketing function — research, content, paid ads, follow-up, attribution — run by AI systems and human operators together. It replaces the marketing-hire path for owner-operator service businesses. AI handles production; human operators set strategy and manage the client relationship. The system installs in 30 days. Everything built belongs to the client. The owner's touchpoint is one 30-minute call per month. Results compound from month one.

### Captured Baseline
URL: https://buildwisemedia.com/playbook/captured-baseline
A captured baseline is an instrumented snapshot of lead flow, attribution, and pipeline taken before any marketing change runs. The Buildwise team instruments referrer source, landing page, and first/last-touch attribution for 30 days before touching anything. Once the window closes, the snapshot is locked with a date stamp and evidence class. Every result reported afterward is a delta above that floor — making lift claims falsifiable rather than aspirational. Every outcome commitment includes a numeric confidence percentage and an evidence class tied to this baseline.

### The Poor Four
URL: https://buildwisemedia.com/playbook/poor-four
The Poor Four are the four lead-system failure modes that kill growth for service businesses: lead drought (no daily acquisition engine), manual mayhem (owner as accidental integration layer across disconnected vendors), the owner trap (business collapses without the founder in the middle), and reactive mode (no instrumentation, so every problem surfaces as a crisis). The four compound — fixing one while the others run leaves the problem intact. The Buildwise install closes all four simultaneously in 30 days.

### Fractional CMO Alternative
URL: https://buildwisemedia.com/playbook/fractional-cmo-alternative
A fractional CMO is an advisor who sets strategy part-time across multiple clients. An AI marketing department runs the full function — research, content, paid acquisition, follow-up, attribution — as a continuous operations layer. The Buildwise Ascend tier family delivers this: Ascend Pilot for early-stage validation, Ascend for done-for-you operations, Ascend Pro for full-scale run-for-you. The distinction matters when execution is the gap, not direction. A fractional CMO is the right hire when an in-house team needs executive leadership. When the owner is the marketing department, an AI department closes the gap a strategist cannot.
